title: First Isomorphism Theorem
statement: Let $f : A \to B$ be any function, and let $\sim$ be the equivalence relation on $A$ defined by $a' \sim a'' \iff f(a') = f(a'')$. Then $f$ decomposes as $A \twoheadrightarrow A/{\sim} \xrightarrow{\sim} \operatorname{im} f \hookrightarrow B$, where the first map is the canonical surjection, the last is the inclusion, and the middle map $\tilde{f} \colon [a]_\sim \mapsto f(a)$ is a bijection.

title: Kuratowski
statement: A graph is planar if and only if it contains no subdivision of $K_5$ or $K_{3,3}$.

title: Ramsey
statement: For positive integers $p_1, \dots, p_n$ and $r \geq 1$, there exists $N$ such that every $n$-coloring of $\binom{[N]}{r}$ admits, for some color $i$, a set of $p_i$ elements all of whose $r$-subsets receive color $i$.

title: Graham-Entringer-Szekely
statement: If $T$ is a spanning tree of the $k$-dimensional cube $Q_k$, then there is an edge of $Q_k$ outside of $T$ whose addition to $T$ creates a cycle of length at least $2k$.
